sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance, forever alone, impossible to duplicate
sketches.resonance: Declaring someone as an object means the language itself guarantees there will only ever be one of them, and no constructor can make another.

sketches.mechanism: A LEFT JOIN where the right side is always NULL — the join condition can never be satisfied, producing only empty matches
sketches.resonance: You reach for every row in the other table but nothing ever matches; the structure of the query enacts the failure to connect.
sketches.draft:
SELECT self.day, other.reply
FROM   days AS self
LEFT JOIN letters AS other
  ON self.sent = other.received
WHERE  other.received IS NULL;

sketches.mechanism: The `freeze` method makes an object permanently immutable — any attempt to modify it raises a RuntimeError at the exact moment of change
sketches.resonance: The last thing you do before letting go is freeze the memory in place, so nothing — not even time — can alter what it was.
sketches.draft:
moment = {
  where: :kitchen,
  light: :golden,
  you:   :laughing
}

moment.freeze

moment[:you] = :gone
# RuntimeError: can't modify frozen Hash
